name: Monk
description: A martial artist of extraordinary focus, channeling an inner wellspring of energy called Focus Points to augment unarmed strikes, deflect attacks, and perform feats beyond ordinary human ability.

properties.value: Martial Weapons that have the Light property

weaponProficiencies: Martial Weapons with the Light property
toolProficiencies: Artisan's Tools or Musical Instrument (one of your choice)

features.name: Martial Arts
features.description:
Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your {rule:Unarmed Strike|Unarmed Strikes} and Monk weapons, which are Simple Melee Weapons and Martial Melee Weapons that have the Light property.

You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

**Bonus Unarmed Strike.** You can make an {rule:Unarmed Strike} as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

**Martial Arts Die.** You can roll 1d6 in place of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk weapons. This die changes as you gain Monk levels: d6 (levels 1–4), d8 (levels 5–10), d10 (levels 11–16), d12 (levels 17–20).

**Dexterous Attacks.** You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. In addition, when you use the Grapple or Shove option of your Unarmed Strike, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine the save DC.

properties.text: While unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and not wearing armor or wielding a Shield: can make an Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action; can roll the Martial Arts die (d6/d8/d10/d12 by level tier) in place of normal damage; can use Dexterity instead of Strength for Unarmed Strike and Monk weapon attack and damage rolls, and for Grapple/Shove DCs.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your base {rule:Armor Class} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers.

modifiers.condition: while not wearing armor or wielding a Shield

features.name: Monk's Focus
features.description:
Your focus and martial training allow you to harness a well of extraordinary energy within yourself. This energy is represented by Focus Points. Your Monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Focus Points column of the Monk Features table (0 at level 1, then equal to your Monk level from level 2 onward).

You can expend these points to enhance or fuel certain Monk features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind, each of which is detailed below.

When you expend a Focus Point, it is unavailable until you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}, at the end of which you regain all your expended points.

Some features that use Focus Points require your target to make a saving throw. The save DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

**Flurry of Blows.** You can expend 1 Focus Point to make two Unarmed Strikes as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

**Patient Defense.** You can take the {action:Disengage} action as a {rule:Bonus Action}. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the {action:Disengage} and the {action:Dodge} actions as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

**Step of the Wind.** You can take the {action:Dash} action as a {rule:Bonus Action}. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the {action:Disengage} and {action:Dash} actions as a {rule:Bonus Action}, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

properties.text: Flurry of Blows (1 Focus Point): make two Unarmed Strikes as a Bonus Action.
Patient Defense: take Disengage as a Bonus Action; or expend 1 Focus Point to take both Disengage and Dodge as a Bonus Action.
Step of the Wind: take Dash as a Bonus Action; or expend 1 Focus Point to take both Disengage and Dash as a Bonus Action, and double jump distance for the turn.

features.name: Unarmored Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain Monk levels: +10 ft (levels 2–5), +15 ft (levels 6–9), +20 ft (levels 10–13), +25 ft (levels 14–17), +30 ft (levels 18–20).

modifiers.condition: while not wearing armor or wielding a Shield

features.name: Uncanny Metabolism
features.description:
When you roll {rule:Initiative}, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: On Initiative roll, you can trigger Uncanny Metabolism: regain all Focus Points and recover HP equal to Monk level + Martial Arts die roll. Once per Long Rest.

features.name: Deflect Attacks
features.description:
When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to reduce the attack's total damage against you. The reduction equals 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier and Monk level.

If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack's force. If you do so, choose a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself if the attack was a melee attack, or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover if the attack was a ranged attack. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.

properties.text: Reaction: when hit by an attack dealing Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, reduce damage by 1d10 + DEX modifier + Monk level. If reduced to 0, can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect: a creature within 5 ft (melee) or 60 ft not behind Total Cover (ranged) must make a DEX save or take 2 × Martial Arts die + DEX modifier damage of the same type.

features.name: Monk Subclass
features.description: You gain a Monk subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monk levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Monk level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Slow Fall
features.description: You can take a {rule:Reaction} when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

properties.text: Reaction: when falling, reduce fall damage by 5 × Monk level.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {action:Attack} action on your turn.

features.name: Stunning Strike
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an {rule:Unarmed Strike}, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {condition:Stunned} condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target's Speed is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against the target before then has {rule:Advantage}.

properties.text: Once per turn when you hit with a Monk weapon or Unarmed Strike, spend 1 Focus Point: target makes a CON save. Fail: Stunned until start of your next turn. Success: speed halved until start of your next turn, and the next attack roll against the target before then has Advantage.

features.name: Empowered Strikes
features.description: Whenever you deal damage with your {rule:Unarmed Strike}, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

properties.text: Unarmed Strike damage can be Force damage instead of its normal type (your choice each time).

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Evasion
features.description:
When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail.

You don't benefit from this feature if you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

properties.text: Against effects that allow a DEX save for half damage: take no damage on a success, half on a failure. Does not apply while Incapacitated.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Acrobatic Movement
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

properties.text: While unarmored and not wielding a Shield, you can move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

features.name: Heightened Focus
features.description:
Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following benefits.

**Flurry of Blows.** You can expend 1 Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows and make three Unarmed Strikes with it instead of two.

**Patient Defense.** When you expend a Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.

**Step of the Wind.** When you expend a Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, you can choose a willing creature within 5 feet of yourself that is Large or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn. The creature's movement doesn't provoke {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks}.

properties.text: Flurry of Blows upgraded: 1 Focus Point now makes three Unarmed Strikes instead of two.
Patient Defense upgraded: spending 1 Focus Point on Patient Defense also grants Temporary Hit Points equal to two Martial Arts die rolls.
Step of the Wind upgraded: spending 1 Focus Point on Step of the Wind lets you carry a willing Large-or-smaller creature with you; its movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Self-Restoration
features.description:
Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: {condition:Charmed}, {condition:Frightened}, or {condition:Poisoned}.

In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn't give you levels of {condition:Exhaustion}.

properties.text: At the end of each of your turns, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.
Forgoing food and drink does not cause Exhaustion levels.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Deflect Energy
features.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

properties.text: Deflect Attacks now works against attacks dealing any damage type.

features.name: Disciplined Survivor
features.description:
Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws.

Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

properties.text: When you fail a saving throw, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it; you must use the new result.

features.name: Perfect Focus
features.description: When you roll {rule:Initiative} and don't use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have 3 or fewer.

properties.text: On Initiative roll, if you have 3 or fewer Focus Points and do not use Uncanny Metabolism, regain Focus Points until you have 4.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Superior Defense
features.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. During that time, you have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to all damage except Force damage.

properties.text: Spend 3 Focus Points at the start of your turn: gain resistance to all damage except Force damage for 1 minute or until Incapacitated.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Irresistible Offense is recommended.

features.name: Body and Mind
features.description: You have developed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

properties.text: Dexterity and Wisdom scores each increase by 4, to a maximum of 25. Recalculate AC (Unarmored Defense), initiative, and other dependent stats.

subclasses.name: Warrior of Mercy
subclasses.description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians, but they bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.

features.name: Warrior of Mercy
features.description:
You gain the following features.

**Hand of Harm.** Once per turn when you hit a creature with an {rule:Unarmed Strike} and deal damage, you can expend 1 Focus Point to deal extra Necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.

**Hand of Healing.** As a {rule:Magic} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore a number of Hit Points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the Unarmed Strikes with a use of this feature without expending a Focus Point for the healing.

**Implements of Mercy.** You gain proficiency in the {rule:Insight} and {rule:Medicine} skills and proficiency with the Herbalism Kit.

properties.text: Hand of Harm: once per turn when you hit with an Unarmed Strike and deal damage, spend 1 Focus Point to deal extra Necrotic damage equal to one Martial Arts die roll + WIS modifier.

Hand of Healing: Magic action, spend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore HP equal to one Martial Arts die roll + WIS modifier. When using Flurry of Blows, can replace one strike with a free use of Hand of Healing.

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description:
Your Hand of Harm and Hand of Healing improve.

**Hand of Harm.** When you use Hand of Harm on a creature, you can also give that creature the {condition:Poisoned} condition until the end of your next turn.

**Hand of Healing.** When you use Hand of Healing, you can also end one of the following conditions on the creature you heal: {condition:Blinded}, {condition:Deafened}, {condition:Paralyzed}, {condition:Poisoned}, or {condition:Stunned}.

properties.text: Hand of Harm upgrade: can also impose Poisoned condition on the target until end of your next turn.
Hand of Healing upgrade: can also end one of Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned from the creature healed.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description:
When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace each of the Unarmed Strikes with a use of Hand of Healing without expending Focus Points for the healing.

In addition, when you make an {rule:Unarmed Strike} with Flurry of Blows and deal damage, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without expending a Focus Point for Hand of Harm. You can still use Hand of Harm only once per turn.

You can use these benefits a total number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: When using Flurry of Blows, can replace any Unarmed Strike with a free Hand of Healing. Can also use Hand of Harm once per turn without spending a Focus Point when hitting with a Flurry of Blows strike. Uses equal to WIS modifier (min 1), recharges on Long Rest.

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description:
Your mastery of life energy opens the door to the ultimate mercy. As a {rule:Magic} action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 Focus Points. The creature then returns to life with a number of Hit Points equal to 4d10 plus your Wisdom modifier. If the creature died with any of the following conditions, the creature revives with those conditions removed: {condition:Blinded}, {condition:Deafened}, {condition:Paralyzed}, {condition:Poisoned}, and {condition:Stunned}.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Magic action, spend 5 Focus Points: touch a corpse that died within 24 hours. The creature returns to life with 4d10 + WIS modifier HP, and revives without the Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned conditions if it died with them. Once per Long Rest.

subclasses.name: Warrior of Shadow
subclasses.description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, harnessing the power of the Shadowfell. They are at home in darkness, able to draw gloom around themselves to hide, leap from shadow to shadow, and take on a wraithlike form.

features.name: Warrior of Shadow
features.description:
You have learned to draw on the power of the Shadowfell, gaining the following benefits.

**Darkness.** You can expend 1 Focus Point to cast the {spell:Darkness} spell without spell components. You can see within the spell's area when you cast it with this feature. While the spell persists, you can move its area of Darkness to a space within 60 feet of yourself at the start of each of your turns.

**Darkvision.** You gain {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

**Shadowy Figments.** You know the {spell:Minor Illusion} spell. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

properties.text: Darkness (1 Focus Point): cast Darkness without components; you can see through your own Darkness; can move the area up to 60 ft at the start of each of your turns while it persists.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: While entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, you can use a {rule:Bonus Action} to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. You then have {rule:Advantage} on the next melee attack you make before the end of the current turn.

properties.text: Bonus Action: while in Dim Light or Darkness, teleport up to 60 ft to another Dim Light or Darkness space you can see. Gain Advantage on the next melee attack roll you make before the end of the turn.

features.name: Improved Shadow Step
features.description: You can draw on your Shadowfell connection to empower your teleportation. When you use your Shadow Step, you can expend 1 Focus Point to remove the requirement that you must start and end in Dim Light or Darkness for that use of the feature. As part of this {rule:Bonus Action}, you can make an {rule:Unarmed Strike} immediately after you teleport.

properties.text: When using Shadow Step, you can spend 1 Focus Point to ignore the Dim Light/Darkness location requirement. You can also make an Unarmed Strike immediately after teleporting as part of the same Bonus Action.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description:
As a {rule:Magic} action while entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, you can expend 3 Focus Points to shroud yourself with shadows for 1 minute, until you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition, or until you end your turn in Bright Light. While shrouded by these shadows, you gain the following benefits.

**Invisibility.** You have the {condition:Invisible} condition.

**Partially Incorporeal.** You can move through occupied spaces as if they were Difficult Terrain. If you end your turn in such a space, you are shunted to the last unoccupied space you were in.

**Shadow Flurry.** You can use your Flurry of Blows without expending any Focus Points.

properties.text: Magic action, spend 3 Focus Points while in Dim Light or Darkness: shroud yourself in shadows for 1 minute (ends early if Incapacitated or you end your turn in Bright Light). While active: you have the Invisible condition; you can move through occupied spaces as Difficult Terrain (shunted out if you end turn there); Flurry of Blows costs no Focus Points.

subclasses.name: Warrior of the Elements
subclasses.description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Harnessing their supernatural focus, these Monks momentarily tame the energy of the Elemental Chaos to empower themselves in and out of battle.

features.name: Warrior of the Elements
features.description:
You gain the following features.

**Elemental Attunement.** At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to imbue yourself with elemental energy. The energy lasts for 10 minutes or until you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. While active:
- **Reach.** When you make an {rule:Unarmed Strike}, your reach is 10 feet greater than normal, as elemental energy extends from you.
- **Elemental Strikes.** Whenever you hit with your Unarmed Strike, you can cause it to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage rather than its normal damage type. When you deal one of these types with it, you can also force the target to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you can move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you.

**Manipulate Elements.** You know the {spell:Elementalism} spell. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

properties.text: Elemental Attunement (1 Focus Point at start of turn): lasts 10 minutes or until Incapacitated. While active: Unarmed Strike reach +10 ft; Unarmed Strikes can deal Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage; when dealing elemental damage, can force a STR save — on fail, move the target up to 10 ft toward or away from you.

features.name: Elemental Burst
features.description:
As a {rule:Magic} action, you can expend 2 Focus Points to cause elemental energy to burst in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within 120 feet of yourself. Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

Each creature in the Sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

properties.text: Magic action, spend 2 Focus Points: elemental burst in a 20-ft-radius Sphere within 120 ft. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Each creature in the area makes a DEX save — fail: 3 × Martial Arts die damage; success: half.

features.name: Stride of the Elements
features.description: While your Elemental Attunement is active, you also have a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

properties.text: While Elemental Attunement is active, gain Fly Speed and Swim Speed each equal to your Speed.

features.name: Elemental Epitome
features.description:
While your Elemental Attunement is active, you also gain the following benefits.

**Damage Resistance.** You gain {rule:Resistance|resistance} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice.

**Destructive Stride.** When you use your Step of the Wind, your Speed increases by 20 feet until the end of the turn. For that duration, any creature of your choice takes damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. The damage type is your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

**Empowered Strikes.** Once on each of your turns, you can deal extra damage to a target equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you hit it with an {rule:Unarmed Strike}. The extra damage is the same type dealt by that strike.

properties.text: While Elemental Attunement is active: resist one of Acid/Cold/Fire/Lightning/Thunder (choose at start of each turn); Step of the Wind grants +20 ft Speed and deals 1 Martial Arts die damage (elemental type of your choice) to each creature you pass within 5 ft (once per creature per turn); once per turn, deal extra Martial Arts die damage of the same type on an Unarmed Strike hit.

subclasses.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and manipulate their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

features.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
features.description:
**Open Hand Technique.** Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target.

**Addle.** The target can't make {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks} until the start of its next turn.

**Push.** The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.

**Topple.** The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {condition:Prone} condition.

properties.text: Open Hand Technique: on each hit granted by Flurry of Blows, impose one effect: Addle (can't make Opportunity Attacks until start of its next turn), Push (STR save or pushed 15 ft), or Topple (DEX save or Prone).

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description:
You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Hit Point regained).

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Bonus Action: roll Martial Arts die and regain HP equal to the roll + WIS modifier (minimum 1 HP). Uses equal to WIS modifier (min 1). Recharges on Long Rest.

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a {rule:Bonus Action} other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that {rule:Bonus Action}.

properties.text: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can immediately use Step of the Wind afterward as well.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description:
You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an {rule:Unarmed Strike}, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you take an action to end them. Alternatively, when you take the {action:Attack} action on your turn, you can forgo one of the attacks to end the vibrations. To end them, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you end them, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action required).

properties.text: When you hit with an Unarmed Strike, spend 4 Focus Points to implant lethal vibrations lasting Monk level days. To trigger: action or forgo one attack from an Attack action (must be on same plane). Target makes a CON save: fail 10d12 Force damage, success half. Only one target at a time. Can dismiss harmlessly for free.
